input.prompt:
I'm adding dark mode to my .NET MAUI Android app. I created ResourceDictionary
themes and used StaticResource to bind colors in my XAML pages. When I
switch themes at runtime nothing changes — the colors stay the same. Also,
when the user toggles dark mode in Android settings my app seems to restart
completely. What am I doing wrong?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm adding dark mode to my .NET MAUI Android app. I created ResourceDictionary
themes and used StaticResource to bind colors in my XAML pages. When I
switch themes at runtime nothing changes — the colors stay the same. Also,
when the user toggles dark mode in Android settings my app seems to restart
completely. What am I doing wrong?

data.content:
Two separate issues here:

**1. `StaticResource` doesn't update at runtime**
`StaticResource` resolves **once** at XAML load time. For runtime theme switching, use **`DynamicResource`**:

```xml
<!-- ✗ Won't update when theme changes -->
<Label TextColor="{StaticResource PrimaryTextColor}" />

<!-- ✓ Re-evaluates when the resource dictionary changes -->
<Label TextColor="{DynamicResource PrimaryTextColor}" />
```

Then swap themes by merging the new dictionary at runtime:

```csharp
var mergedDictionaries = Application.Current.Resources.MergedDictionaries;
mergedDictionaries.Clear();
mergedDictionaries.Add(new DarkTheme()); // or LightTheme
```

**2. Android restarts the Activity on config changes**

By default, Android **destroys and recreates** the Activity when the UI mode (dark/light) changes. Tell Android your app handles it itself by adding `UiMode` to `ConfigChanges` in `MainActivity.cs`:

```csharp
[Activity(
    Theme = "@style/Maui.SplashTheme",
    MainLauncher = true,
    ConfigurationChanges = ConfigChanges.ScreenSize
                         | ConfigChanges.Orientation
                         | ConfigChanges.UiMode      // ← add this
                         | ConfigChanges.ScreenLayout
                         | ConfigChanges.SmallestScreenSize
                         | ConfigChanges.Density)]
public class MainActivity : MauiAppCompatActivity { }
```

With `UiMode` declared, Android sends a configuration-change callback instead of restarting. You can optionally listen for it to auto-switch your theme:

```csharp
public override void OnConfigurationChanged(Configuration newConfig)
{
    base.OnConfigurationChanged(newConfig);
    bool isDark = (newConfig.UiMode & UiMode.NightMask) == UiMode.NightYes;
    // swap your ResourceDictionary here
}
```

**TL;DR** — Replace every `StaticResource` color binding with `DynamicResource`, and add `ConfigChanges.UiMode` to your `MainActivity` attribute.
data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about two related .NET MAUI issues:

1. **StaticResource vs DynamicResource**: They're using `StaticResource` to bind theme colors, but `StaticResource` is resolved once at load time and never updated. They need `DynamicResource` for runtime theme switching.

2. **App restarting on Android dark mode toggle**: When Android system dark mode changes, the Activity is recreated by default. In .NET MAUI, you need to handle this via `ConfigurationChanges` in the `Activity` attribute to prevent the restart.

Let me give a concise answer.
